'''Flalios''' was a [[Gladiator/Legends|gladiator]] in [[the Cauldron]] arena on [[Rattatak/Legends|Rattatak]], where he used twin ball-and-chain weapons against other fighters. Shortly after the outbreak of the [[Clone Wars/Legends|Clone Wars]], Flalios was killed by falling rocks caused by [[Asajj Ventress/Legends|Asajj Ventress]].

==Biography==
Flalios<ref name="Sticker">''[[Ultimate Sticker Book: Star Wars: Clone Wars]]''</ref> was one of the many [[Gladiator/Legends|gladiators]] who tried to win their freedom from the arenas of [[Rattatak/Legends|Rattatak]] during the final years of the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Old Republic]].<ref name="CE" /> Shortly after the outbreak of the [[Clone Wars/Legends|Clone Wars]],<ref name="NEC">''[[The New Essential Chronology]]''</ref> Flalios, on this way, ended up in [[the Cauldron]] arena.<ref name="CW" /> It was the largest gladiatorial pit on the [[Planet/Legends|planet]], filled with violent warriors, where war barons and generals sought out the best combatants who would win their wars.<ref name="DBR">{{DB|location|rattatak|Rattatak}}</ref> Therefore, Flalios, like many other gladiators,<ref name="CW" /> entered a deadly free-for-all battle,<ref name="EGD">''[[The New Essential Guide to Droids]]''</ref> but it was a rare gladiator indeed who could survive multiple fights there.<ref name="DBR" />

[[File:Flalios_Swyym-Ee.jpg|thumb|left|200px|Flalios faces off against Swyy'm-Ee]]
During one battle, the [[Dark Lord of the Sith/Legends|Sith Lord]] [[Count of Serenno/Legends|Count]] [[Dooku/Legends|Dooku]] attended the match, hoping to find a new [[Sith apprentice/Legends|apprentice]] among the fighters. Flalios fought<ref name="CW" /> the [[Trogodile/Legends|Trogodile]] [[Swyy'm-Ee]]<ref name="Sticker" /> when the [[Dathomirian/Legends|Dathomirian]] [[Asajj Ventress/Legends|Asajj Ventress]] entered the arena and, in a short time, defeated most of the remaining gladiators. The survivors united for a single attack against Ventress, but she used the [[The Force/Legends|the Force]] to crush stone debris from the ceiling onto their heads.<ref name="CW" />

==Personality==
Flalios often fought with chains, whipping them around to deliver stunning blows to the heads of his opponents.<ref name="CE" />

==Behind the scenes==
Flalios<ref name="Sticker" /> first appeared as an unnamed background character in the [[Chapter 6 (Star Wars: Clone Wars)|sixth chapter]]<ref name="CW" /> of ''[[Star Wars: Clone Wars]]'', an animated television micro-series<ref name="Chapter Archive">{{SWArchive|url=clonewars/microseries/chapter/archive.html|text=View ''Clone Wars'' Chapter Archive}}</ref> produced by [[Cartoon Network]] Studios<ref name="Volume One">[[Star Wars: Clone Wars Volume One|''Star Wars: Clone Wars'' Volume One]]</ref> and originally aired on [[November 14]], [[2003]].<ref name="TV">{{TVcom|url=shows/star-wars-clone-wars/chapter-vi-276612|text=Star Wars: Clone Wars - Season 1, Episode 6: Chapter VI}}</ref> The character was later given a name in ''[[Ultimate Sticker Book: Star Wars: Clone Wars]]'', a sticker book written by [[Simon Beecroft]] and released in [[2004]].<ref name="DK">{{DK|url=us/9780756607593-ultimate-sticker-book-star-wars-clone-wars|text=Ultimate Sticker Book: Star Wars: Clone Wars|nolive=1}}</ref> However, in later sources, neither Flalios's backstory nor even origin was revealed.

The name of this gladiator was phonetically similar to [[wikipedia:en:flail (weapon)|flail]], a weapon consisting of a striking head attached to a handle by a flexible rope, strap, or chain. Flalios's appearance was borrowed with minor modifications from the character [[Malakili/Legends|Malakili]] from ''[[Star Wars: Episode VI – Return of the Jedi]]''.
